name: Signup flow dropping 60% at step 2
description: Analytics show: step 1 (email + password) → 100%, step 2 (company info + team size) → 40%, step 3 (billing card) → 35%, complete → 30%. So 60% drop between step 1 and step 2. The step 2 asks: company name, company website, number of employees, industry, use case, referral source, job title.
task:
Our signup flow drops 60% between 'enter email + password' and 'company info' step. Step 2 currently asks 7 questions: company name, company website, # employees, industry, use case, referral source, job title.

What exact changes should we make to rescue this conversion rate? Be specific:
1. Which questions should we cut/defer/keep and why?
2. Order the remaining questions by priority
3. Should we gate the core value behind this or let users in first?
4. What UX changes help (progress indicator, save-and-continue, optional fields)?
5. Write the new step 2 UI copy (labels, helper text, button text)

Target: recover at least 20 percentage points of conversion at this step.
expected_outcomes: Specific questions identified as deferrable vs essential
Reasoning for each cut (friction vs value)
Activation-first vs info-first tradeoff explicitly addressed
New copy drafted for the revised step
